title: abrotanella submarginata
intro: Abrotanella submarginata is a flowering plant species in the family Compositae, commonly known as New Zealand celery pine or submarginata. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly by the indigenous Māori people, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Abrotanella submarginata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Abrotanella submarginata can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its essential oils can be extracted for use in aromatherapy and topical applications.
